cognitive_layer.purpose: Performs structure-based molecular docking validation on candidate target proteins identified from single-cell ligand-receptor analysis. Accepts pre-processed target protein list (from CellChat differential analysis) and a compound library, then uses AutoDock Vina to rank binding affinities and generate docking poses. This bridges the gap between single-cell signaling inference and therapeutic compound prioritization.

parameter_impact.target_proteins: List of UniProt IDs or gene symbols for proteins to dock against. If null, automatically extracts top differential LR-pair proteins from input data analysis history.
parameter_impact.compound_library: 'fda_approved' uses the built-in FDA drug library; 'custom_cas' requires custom_cas_list; 'custom_dir' requires pre-processed PDBQT files in custom_compound_dir.
parameter_impact.docking_exhaustiveness: Higher values increase search accuracy but scale quadratically in runtime. Default 8 is sufficient for screening.
parameter_impact.drug_info_csv: Optional path to BindingDB-style annotation CSV for adding known pharmacological evidence to results.

error_handling.FileNotFoundError: protein structure: Check that UniProt IDs are correct and network access to AlphaFold/PDB is available.
error_handling.FileNotFoundError: compound: Verify compound library path or CAS numbers are valid and PubChem access is available.
error_handling.OSError: AutoDock Vina not found: AutoDock Vina must be installed and in PATH. Install: conda install -c bioconda autodock-vina

too_many_targets.adjust: Limit to top 5-10 by absolute LR communication probability difference
too_many_targets.causal_chain: Docking is computationally expensive (scales with n_targets × n_compounds × exhaustiveness). Screening too many targets simultaneously reduces depth of search per target.
too_many_targets.expected_effect: Higher quality binding pose predictions per target

low_confidence_poses.causal_chain: Higher exhaustiveness performs more iterations of the Vina local search algorithm, improving the probability of finding the global minimum.
low_confidence_poses.expected_effect: More reproducible and energetically favorable binding poses
